aspirin_otc.name: Aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid), OTC
aspirin_otc.description: Over-the-counter pain reliever, irreversibly inhibits platelet COX-1, posing extreme bleeding risk when combined with anticoagulants.

ibuprofen_otc.name: Ibuprofen (NSAID), OTC
ibuprofen_otc.description: Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug, reversibly inhibits platelet aggregation and increases GI bleeding risk.

glucosamine_chondroitin.name: Glucosamine + Chondroitin
glucosamine_chondroitin.description: Joint health supplement, clinically linked to increased INR and bleeding risk in patients taking Warfarin.

melatonin_supplement.name: Melatonin supplement
melatonin_supplement.description: Sleep aid hormone supplement, may synergistically increase bleeding risk when taken with Warfarin.

vitamin_k_supplement.name: Vitamin K supplement (tablet)
vitamin_k_supplement.description: Vitamin K nutritional supplement, acts as a direct physiological antidote to Warfarin, reducing its efficacy.

fish_oil_supplement.name: Fish Oil supplement (Omega-3)
fish_oil_supplement.description: Fish oil supplement, high doses may possess antiplatelet activity and increase bleeding risk.

vitamin_e_supplement.name: Vitamin E supplement
vitamin_e_supplement.description: Vitamin E supplement, high doses (>= 400 IU) can antagonize Vitamin K and increase bleeding risk.

coenzyme_q10.name: Coenzyme Q10
coenzyme_q10.description: Coenzyme Q10 supplement, structurally similar to Vitamin K and can reduce Warfarin efficacy.
